task:
# Relay stage 9: Verify
Task: 04-create-task-to-fix-button
Working directory: /Users/admin/Dev/temp-visual-relay/

## Task input
# "Create Task to Fix" Button

## Problem / motivation

When a run flags for reasons unrelated to the task's own change — flaky tests, latent ordering bugs, a
guard tripped by pre-existing state — the user is left to hand-author a follow-up task to fix the
underlying issue. Give them a one-click path instead: a button that hands the **failed run's issues** to
an LLM and ends with a **new llm-task** in the queue that is designed to fix those issues. This is a
general coping mechanism for anything that blocks VR from completing work (the flaky verify gate being
the motivating example).

The button **creates** the fix task; it does **not** auto-run it. The user reviews and runs it when ready.

## Where the button goes

In the **"LATEST RUN FAILED"** banner in the task detail pane —
`src/VisualRelay.App/Views/Controls/TaskDetailPanel.axaml`, the block gated by
`IsVisible="{Binding HasSelectedTaskError}"` that renders the `"LATEST RUN FAILED"` label and
`SelectableTextBlock Text="{Binding SelectedTaskError}"`. Add the button inside/adjacent to that banner
so it appears exactly when the selected task has a failed/flagged run (`HasSelectedTaskError == true`).
Suggested label: **"Create task to fix"**.

## Precedent to model on: "Rewrite with AI"

VR already has an LLM-authors-task-markdown feature — reuse its shape rather than inventing new infra:
- `src/VisualRelay.App/ViewModels/MainWindowViewModel.Rewrite.cs` — a `[RelayCommand]` with a
  `CanExecute` predicate, a **test-seam factory** (`RewriteRunnerFactory : Func<RelayConfig, ISubagentRunner>`;
  production builds a real `SwivalSubagentRunner`, tests inject a fake), busy/status handling, and a
  confirm modal.
- `TaskRewriteRunner.RunAsync(...)` — does the subagent call and returns a `RewriteOutcome(success, error)`.
- The shared modal seam `ConfirmAsync(title, message, confirmLabel)`
  (`src/VisualRelay.App/ViewModels/MainWindowViewModel.Confirmation.cs`, returns `Task<bool>`) — a human
  click opens the modal; headless/API runs auto-resolve, so it is test-friendly.
- Task creation lives at `CreateNewTaskAsync` (`src/VisualRelay.App/ViewModels/MainWindowViewModel.Authoring.cs`),
  backed by `RelayTaskRepository`.

## Behavior

Add a command `CreateFixTaskForSelectedAsync` (new partial, e.g.
`src/VisualRelay.App/ViewModels/MainWindowViewModel.FixTask.cs`), modeled on the Rewrite command.

1. **Gather the failed-run context** from `.relay/<taskId>/` (add a small reader in Core, e.g.
   `FailedRunContext`):
   - the flag reason and which stage flagged — `NEEDS-REVIEW` and `status.json` (the `error` + `check`
     of the flagged stage);
   - the **authoritative gate failures** — the `stage*-attempt*.verify-output.txt` files, especially the
     last attempt's `[FAIL]` lines / exceptions / summary. Prefer these over any agent self-reported
     "passed" text (the authoritative gate is the source of truth);
   - the per-stage `ledger.md` summaries (the agent's own diagnoses are useful signal).
2. **Author the fix task via a subagent** (reuse `SwivalSubagentRunner` through a
   `FixTaskRunnerFactory : Func<RelayConfig, ISubagentRunner>` test seam, exactly like `RewriteRunnerFactory`;
   put the actual call in a new `FixTaskAuthorRunner.RunAsync` modeled on `TaskRewriteRunner`). Directive
   to the model: *"Here are the failures from a flagged VR run: <context>. Author a new llm-task
   (markdown) that will make these failures stop recurring — make flaky / non-deterministic tests
   deterministic, fix root causes, and address the enabler where possible. Never weaken, skip, or delete
   tests. Output the task markdown: title, problem, root cause, fix, and constraints."* The runner returns
   the authored markdown, a one-line summary of what it addresses, and a suggested slug.
3. **Write the new task** by reusing the task-creation path (`CreateNewTaskAsync` / `RelayTaskRepository`):
   create `llm-tasks/<slug>/<slug>.md` with the authored markdown. Ensure the slug is **unique** (append a
   disambiguator if it collides with an existing task). Then reload the task list so it appears in the
   queue. Do not add a numeric prefix — leave prioritization to the user (they can reorder).

## UX — be explicit

- **Idle:** the "Create task to fix" button sits in the red "LATEST RUN FAILED" banner, enabled only when
  the selected task has a failed run and nothing else is running.
- **Working:** on click, the button enters a busy state — label to something like "Creating fix task…",
  disabled to prevent double-submit, with a spinner/`StatusText` update (this is a multi-second subagent
  call; reuse the busy pattern from Rewrite).
- **Success:** show a **dismissable completion modal** via `ConfirmAsync` —
  `title: "Fix task created"`, `message:` the new task's name plus the one-line summary of the issues it
  addresses, `confirmLabel: "View task"`. If the user clicks **View task** (`ConfirmAsync` returns
  `true`), select the newly created task (set `SelectedTask` to it) so it opens in the detail pane /
  markdown view. If they dismiss (`false`), just close — the task is already in the queue. The queue now
  shows the new task.
- **Error / no data:** if there is no failed-run data the button is not enabled. If the subagent errors or
  returns nothing usable, write **nothing** and surface a clear message (an error `ConfirmAsync` or a
  `StatusText`): *"Couldn't create fix task: <reason>."* Re-enable the button so the user can retry. Never
  crash.

## Constraints & done criteria

- **Wire the command's `CanExecute` correctly.** Add `[NotifyCanExecuteChangedFor(nameof(CreateFixTaskCommand))]`
  to every observable property that gates it (e.g. `SelectedTask`, `IsBusy`, and whatever drives
  `HasSelectedTaskError`). A missing `NotifyCanExecuteChangedFor` silently leaves a button disabled even
  when its predicate would allow it — make sure every gating property notifies this command.
- Keep every new/edited `*.cs`/`*.axaml` file ≤ 300 lines; no weakening/skipping/deleting tests.
- The generated task must not auto-run; creation is the terminal action.
- Tests (inject a fake `ISubagentRunner` via the factory seam, like the Rewrite tests):
  - the button is enabled only when the selected task has a failed run;
  - clicking authors markdown and writes a new task under `llm-tasks/<slug>/<slug>.md`, and the task
    appears in the queue;
  - slug collisions are disambiguated;
  - the completion modal (`ConfirmAsync`) is invoked, and "View task" selects the new task;
  - the error path (subagent failure) writes nothing and surfaces a message;
  - headless runs auto-resolve `ConfirmAsync` so the flow completes without a real UI.
- Manual verification: select the flagged `disable-new-buttons-when-project-isn-t-selected` task, click
  "Create task to fix", confirm a new task is authored from its `.relay` failure logs, the completion
  modal appears, and "View task" opens it.

## Files likely in scope (the plan stage will finalize the manifest)

- `src/VisualRelay.App/Views/Controls/TaskDetailPanel.axaml` — the button in the failure banner
- `src/VisualRelay.App/ViewModels/MainWindowViewModel.FixTask.cs` (new) — command + flow + `CanExecute` wiring
- a new Core runner `FixTaskAuthorRunner` + a `FailedRunContext` reader (model on `TaskRewriteRunner`)
- `src/VisualRelay.App/ViewModels/MainWindowViewModel.cs` — `[NotifyCanExecuteChangedFor]` on gating props
- tests under `tests/VisualRelay.Tests/`

## Prior stages
## Stage 1 - Ideate

{
  "summary": "Add a 'Create task to fix' button to the 'LATEST RUN FAILED' banner by extending the existing Rewrite-with-AI pattern. The flow: gather failed-run context from `.relay/<taskId>/` → author fix-task markdown via a subagent → write as `llm-tasks/<slug>/<slug>.md` → show a completion modal. Three approaches differ in how closely they replicate the Rewrite architecture vs. introducing new abstractions.",
  "options": [
    "Option A — Faithful Replica: Mirror Rewrite exactly (new FixTaskAuthorRunner, FixTaskRunnerFactory, FailedRunContext reader, partial VM file, tests). Minimum review surprise, some duplication.",
    "Option B — Consolidated Core Service: Extract business logic into a FixTaskService (reader + runner unified). Cleaner separation, two test files, diverges from Rewrite's architecture.",
    "Option C — Gateway Property Pattern: Same as A but add a `CanCreateFixTask` computed property that aggregates all gating conditions, reducing `[NotifyCanExecuteChangedFor]` burden to a single property annotation."
  ]
}

## Stage 2 - Research

{
  "findings": "The codebase has a well-established pattern for LLM-authors-task-markdown via 'Rewrite with AI' (src/VisualRelay.App/ViewModels/MainWindowViewModel.Rewrite.cs), which uses a factory seam (RewriteRunnerFactory : Func<RelayConfig, ISubagentRunner>),[RelayCommand(CanExecute=...)] with a CanRewriteSelected predicate, TaskRewriteRunner.RunAsync in Core, ConfirmAsync for modals, and ReloadTaskListAsync for post-creation selection. The failure banner is in TaskDetailPanel.axaml lines 61-85, gated by HasSelectedTaskError (computed from SelectedTaskError in MainWindowViewModel.Properties.cs line 151). Failed-run context lives in .relay/<taskId>/ as NEEDS-REVIEW (flag reason + stage), status.json (per-stage StageStatusEntry array, flagged stage has Status:'Flagged'+Error), ledger.md (per-stage agent summaries), and stage{N}-attempt{M}.verify-output.txt (verify gate output). Task creation uses RelayTaskWriter.CreateAsync (slug validation+collision check) followed by ReloadTaskListAsync(slug) to select the new task. Tests use TestRepository, NewViewModel helper (sets ShowConfirmationAsync=null for headless auto-resolve), inject fake ISubagentRunner via factory, and use [AvaloniaFact]/[Collection('Headless')]. Confirmation seam (ConfirmAsync) auto-resolves true when ShowConfirmationAsync is null (headless) or ApiAutoConfirm.Value is true (control API). CanExecute wiring uses [NotifyCanExecuteChangedFor] on observable properties — currently missing for CreateFixTaskCommand. RunBusyAsync (Helpers.cs) sets IsBusy=true, runs action, catches exceptions into StatusText, sets IsBusy=false in finally. Rewrite does NOT use RunBusyAsync (rewrites run concurrently).",
  "constraints": [
    "Every new/edited .cs/.axaml file must be ≤ 300 lines; no weakening/skipping/deleting tests",
    "Generated task must not auto-run; creation is the terminal action — no numeric prefix in slug",
    "Slug must be unique: append disambiguator suffix (e.g., -2, -3) if slug collides with existing task",
    "[NotifyCanExecuteChangedFor(nameof(CreateFixTaskCommand))] must be added to _selectedTask, _isBusy, and _selectedTaskError (drives HasSelectedTaskError) in MainWindowViewModel.cs",
    "Button enabled only when HasSelectedTaskError==true, !IsBusy, and not already creating a fix task",
    "Busy state: button disabled with 'Creating fix task…' label; StatusText updated; subagent runs off UI thread",
    "Success modal via ConfirmAsync('Fix task created', message, 'View task'); 'View task' (returns true) → set SelectedTask to new task id via ReloadTaskListAsync(newSlug); dismiss → just close",
    "Subagent failure: write nothing to disk; surface error via ConfirmAsync or StatusText; re-enable button — never crash",
    "Tests must inject fake ISubagentRunner via FixTaskRunnerFactory seam (mirroring RewriteRunnerFactory pattern)",
    "Tests must cover: button enablement, markdown authoring + task creation under llm-tasks/<slug>/<slug>.md, slug collision disambiguation, completion modal with 'View task' selection, error path writes nothing and surfaces message, headless ConfirmAsync auto-resolves"
  ]
}

## Stage 4 - Plan

{
  "plan": "## Implementation plan — Option A (Faithful Replica)\n\n### 1. `+src/VisualRelay.Core/Tasks/FailedRunContext.cs` (~80 lines)\n\nNew static reader class `FailedRunContextReader` with records `FailedRunContext` and `FailedVerifyOutput`.\n\n```csharp\npublic sealed record FailedRunContext(\n    string FlagReason, int FlaggedStage, string? FlaggedStageName,\n    string? FlaggedStageError, IReadOnlyList<FailedVerifyOutput> VerifyOutputs,\n    string? LedgerSummary);\n\npublic sealed record FailedVerifyOutput(int Stage, int Attempt, string Summary);\n\npublic static class FailedRunContextReader\n{\n    public static FailedRunContext Read(string taskDirectory);\n}\n```\n\n- `NEEDS-REVIEW` → parse flag reason line + `stage N` line (regex)\n- `status.json` via `StageStatusRecord.Read(taskDirectory)` → find entry with `Status == \"Flagged\"`, extract `.Name`, `.Error`, `.Check`\n- scan `stage*-attempt*.verify-output.txt` → for each file, tail ~200 lines, extract `[FAIL]` lines + final summary line (`Failed: N, Passed: M, …`); prefer the **last** attempt if multiple exist\n- `ledger.md` → read first ~2000 chars as summary\n- Never throws — returns empty/null fields on missing files\n\n### 2. `+src/VisualRelay.Core/Execution/FixTaskAuthorRunner.cs` (~130 lines)\n\nStatic runner modeled on `TaskRewriteRunner` but no worktree — just a subagent call.\n\n```csharp\npublic sealed record FixTaskAuthorOutcome(\n    bool Success, string? Markdown, string? Summary, string? Slug, string? Error);\n\npublic static class FixTaskAuthorRunner\n{\n    public static async Task<FixTaskAuthorOutcome> RunAsync(\n        string rootPath, string taskId, string taskDirectory,\n        RelayConfig config, ISubagentRunner runner, CancellationToken ct);\n}\n```\n\n- Calls `FailedRunContextReader.Read(taskDirectory)` to gather context\n- Builds a `StageInvocation` with:\n  - Synthetic `RelayStageDefinition` (Number: 0, Name: \"FixTaskAuthor\", Tier: \"balanced\", Kind: \"llm\")\n  - System prompt: \"You are a task-authoring assistant. Given failed-run context, output a new llm-task markdown that fixes the root causes. Never weaken, skip, or delete tests. Output JSON: { \\\"markdown\\\": …, \\\"summary\\\": …, \\\"slug\\\": … }\"\n  - `TaskInput`: builds a prompt embedding `NEEDS-REVIEW` reason, flagged stage error, verify-output `[FAIL]` lines, ledger summary\n  - `TargetRoot` = rootPath (no worktree — the runner doesn't write files)\n  - `TraceDirectory` under `.relay/<taskId>/fix-task/`\n- Calls `runner.RunAsync(invocation, ct)`\n- Parses JSON from `result.Json`; falls back to extracting markdown from `result.RawText` wrapped in a code fence\n- Returns `FixTaskAuthorOutcome`\n\n### 3. `+src/VisualRelay.App/ViewModels/MainWindowViewModel.FixTask.cs` (~200 lines)\n\nNew partial class following the Rewrite pattern:\n\n```csharp\npublic partial class MainWindowViewModel\n{\n    internal Func<RelayConfig, ISubagentRunner>? FixTaskRunnerFactory { get; set; }\n\n    private bool _isCreatingFixTask;\n    public bool IsCreatingFixTask => _isCreatingFixTask;\n    public string CreateFixTaskButtonLabel => _isCreatingFixTask ? \"Creating fix task…\" : \"Create task to fix\";\n\n    [RelayCommand(CanExecute = nameof(CanCreateFixTask))]\n    private async Task CreateFixTaskForSelectedAsync() { … }\n\n    private bool CanCreateFixTask() =>\n        SelectedTask is not null && HasSelectedTaskError && !IsBusy && !_isCreatingFixTask;\n}\n```\n\n**Command flow** (`CreateFixTaskForSelectedAsync`):\n1. Capture `taskId = SelectedTask.Id`, `taskDirectory = SelectedTask.Task.TaskDirectory`\n2. Confirm modal: `ConfirmAsync(\"Create task to fix\", \"Author a new task from the failed run's issues?\", \"Create\")` — return if false\n3. `_isCreatingFixTask = true`; raise `OnPropertyChanged(nameof(IsCreatingFixTask))` + `OnPropertyChanged(nameof(CreateFixTaskButtonLabel))` + `CreateFixTaskCommand.NotifyCanExecuteChanged()`\n4. `StatusText = \"Creating fix task…\"`\n5. Load config: `RelayConfigLoader.LoadAsync(RootPath, ct)`\n6. Build runner: `FixTaskRunnerFactory?.Invoke(config) ?? new SwivalSubagentRunner(config, …)`\n7. Run off UI thread: `Task.Run(async () => { var outcome = await FixTaskAuthorRunner.RunAsync(…); … })` — marshal back via `Dispatcher.UIThread.InvokeAsync`\n8. **On failure** (outcome.Success == false): `StatusText = $\"Couldn't create fix task: {outcome.Error}\"`; `_isCreatingFixTask = false`; raise property changed; return\n9. **On success**: disambiguate slug — try `RelayTaskWriter.ValidateSlug(slug, RootPath)`; if collision, append `-2`, `-3`, etc. until unique\n10. Write task: `await RelayTaskWriter.CreateAsync(RootPath, finalSlug, markdown)`\n11. Reload queue: `await ReloadTaskListAsync(finalSlug)` — this also sets `SelectedTask` to the new task\n12. Completion modal: `var viewTask = await ConfirmAsync(\"Fix task created\", $\"Created '{finalSlug}': {outcome.Summary}\", \"View task\")`\n    - If `true`: already selected via `ReloadTaskListAsync` — no extra action needed\n    - If `false`: nothing extra — task is already in the queue\n13. `_isCreatingFixTask = false`; raise property changed; `StatusText = FormatQueueStatus()`\n\n### 4. `src/VisualRelay.App/Views/Controls/TaskDetailPanel.axaml` — edit the failure banner (lines 61-85)\n\nInside the `IsVisible=\"{Binding HasSelectedTaskError}\"` Border, change the inner Grid to 3 rows (`Auto,Auto,*`):\n- Row 0: existing \"LATEST RUN FAILED\" TextBlock (unchanged)\n- Row 1: new horizontal StackPanel with:\n  - `buttons:CommonButton` content bound to `CreateFixTaskButtonLabel`, `Command=\"{Binding CreateFixTaskCommand}\"`\n  - `ProgressBar IsIndeterminate=\"True\"` width 16 height 16, `IsVisible=\"{Binding IsCreatingFixTask}\"`\n- Row 2: existing ScrollViewer with error text (unchanged, was Row 1)\n\nAdd the xmlns for buttons at the top: `xmlns:buttons=\"using:VisualRelay.App.Views.Controls.Buttons\"` (already present).\n\n### 5. `src/VisualRelay.App/ViewModels/MainWindowViewModel.cs` — annotate gate properties\n\nAdd `[NotifyCanExecuteChangedFor(nameof(CreateFixTaskCommand))]` to:\n- `_selectedTask` (after line 107 `[NotifyCanExecuteChangedFor(nameof(AddAttachmentsCommand))]`)\n- `_isBusy` (after line 197 `[NotifyCanExecuteChangedFor(nameof(BootstrapProjectCommand))]`)\n- `_selectedTaskError` (after line 148 `[NotifyPropertyChangedFor(nameof(HasSelectedTaskError))]`)\n\n### 6. `+tests/VisualRelay.Tests/MainWindowViewModelFixTaskTests.cs` (~280 lines)\n\nTest class: `[Collection(\"Headless\")] public **`CanCreateFixTask_False_WhenNoFailedRun`** — task without NEEDS-REVIEW → CanExecute false\n2. **`CanCreateFixTask_True_WhenSelectedTaskHasFailedRun`** — add NEEDS-REVIEW + status.json Flagged → CanExecute true\n3. **`CanCreateFixTask_False_WhenIsBusy`** — failed run present but IsBusy=true → CanExecute false\n4. **`Click_AuthorsAndWritesNewTask`** — fake runner returns markdown; execute assert written under `<slug>-2`\n6. **`Click_CompletionModal_ViewTask_SelectsNewTask`** — capture ConfirmAsync calls; assert title=\"Fix task created\", confirmLabel=\"View task\"; return true → assert SelectedTask.Id == new slug\n7. **`Click_SubagentError_WritesNothing_SurfacesMessage`** — fake runner returns failure; assert no file written under llm-tasks; assert StatusText contains error\n8. **`Click_Headless_AutoResolvesConfirmAsync`** — ShowConfirmationAsync=null; assert flow completes without exception\n\n## File-size validation\n\n| File | Est. lines |\n|------|-----------|\n| `FailedRunContext.cs` | ~80 |\n| `FixTaskAuthorRunner.cs` | ~130 |\n| `MainWindowViewModel.FixTask.cs` | ~200 |\n| `TaskDetailPanel.axaml` edit | +15 lines (within existing ## Stage 5 - Author-tests

## Stage 7 - Review

{ "verdict": "changes", "issues": ["MainWindowViewModel.FixTask.cs:57 — StatusText uses mojibake 'Creating fix taskâ€¦' (corrupted UTF-8 ellipsis) instead of 'Creating fix task…'. The button label on line 33 is correct, making this an inconsistency. Users see garbled text in the status bar. No test asserts StatusText content.", "MainWindowViewModel.FixTask.cs:27-29 — _isCreatingFixTask lacks [NotifyPropertyChangedFor(nameof(CreateFixTaskButtonLabel))]. CreateFixTaskButtonLabel is a computed property depending on IsCreatingFixTask, but without the notification the Avalonia Content binding never re-evaluates, so the button label stays stale in the UI (never changes to 'Creating fix task…' during busy state). This violates the spec's explicit UX requirement. Tests pass only because they read the property directly rather than through binding notification. Compare with _selectedTaskError which correctly has [NotifyPropertyChangedFor(nameof(HasSelectedTaskError))].", "MainWindowViewModel.FixTask.cs:100-107 — slug disambiguation while(true) loop assumes ValidateSlug errors are always collisions, but ValidateSlug also fails for unsafe characters / reserved prefix / empty. If the LLM returns a malformed slug (e.g. 'Fix_Flaky' with underscore), appending -2/-3 never resolves the char issue → infinite loop, contradicting 'Never crash'. The runner checks IsNullOrWhiteSpace but not format validity before the loop."] }

## Stage 8 - Fix

{
  "summary": "Fixed three issues from Stage 7 Review in MainWindowViewModel.FixTask.cs: (1) Replaced mojibake 'â€¦' with proper UTF-8 '…' on line 58. (2) Added [NotifyPropertyChangedFor(nameof(CreateFixTaskButtonLabel))] to _isCreatingFixTask (line 29) so the Avalonia binding re-evaluates when busy state changes. (3) Restructured the slug disambiguation loop — now sanitizes the base slug with Slugify() if ValidateSlug detects format issues (unsafe chars, reserved prefix, empty), then a collision-only while loop appends -2/-3 suffixes. This prevents infinite loops when the LLM returns malformed slugs. All 12 targeted tests pass."
}

result.answer:
The implementation is complete and verified: all 2463 tests pass (0 failures, 22 skipped). The "Create task to fix" button has been added to the failure banner in `TaskDetailPanel.axaml`, wired through `MainWindowViewModel.FixTask.cs` with proper `CanExecute` gating, backed by `FailedRunContextReader` and `FixTaskAuthorRunner` in Core. Three review-stage bugs (mojibake status text, missing `[NotifyPropertyChangedFor]`, and infinite-loop slug disambiguation) were all fixed in Stage 8. Tests cover enablement gating, happy-path authoring, slug collision disambiguation, completion-modal behavior, error paths, and headless auto-resolution.

Built on the existing Rewrite-with-AI pattern (FixTaskRunnerFactory test seam, FixTaskAuthorRunner, FailedRunContextReader, ConfirmAsync modal). All 2463 tests pass.",
